opinion.h4: HALL v. ILLINOIS. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF ILLINOIS. No. 848, Misc. Decided March 8, 1965.
opinion.p: The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
380 U.S. 250 ARTHUR v. COLORADO. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE SUPREME COURT OF COLORADO. No. 486, Misc. Decided March 8, 1965.
Duke W. Dunbar, Attorney General of Colorado, Frank E. Hickey, Deputy Attorney General, and John E. Bush, Assistant Attorney General, for respondent.

The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for a writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is reversed. Gideon v. Wainwright, 372 U.S. 335; Pickelsimer v. Wainwright, 375 U.S. 2.
